attacking_style: Does not initiate attacks from distance; capitalizes on late-race positioning chaos or disorganization in sprint finales, converting leadout duties into opportunistic finishes when openings emerge.
team_tactics: Primary role as high-powered leadout domestique delivering extended, field-shattering pace in final kilometers. Team leverages his ability to fracture rival sprint trains through sustained tempo.
terrain_preference: Flat sprint stages where sustained high pace serves dual purpose: leadout work and personal positioning. Classical one-day parcours suit podium capability when reduced bunch arrives.
vulnerability: Lacks explosive acceleration for pure sprint duels when caught by faster finishers. Neutralized when forced to attack early against alert opposition on selective terrain.
key_patterns: Extended high-pace leadouts fracture rival sprint train organization (Vuelta stages, 2 observations)
Converts from domestique to opportunistic winner when lead sprinter fails/disappears (Vuelta 2021)
Podium capable in classics but lacks winning punch when neutralized early (Bretagne 2020)
Finishing speed exceeds typical leadout domestique expectations, dual-threat capability evident
form_trajectory: positive: Successfully transitioned from neutralized classic attempt (2020) to dominant leadout performances with opportunistic Vuelta win (2021), showing tactical evolution
